expectations.text: Emits a machine-readable structured plan (JSON with a moves array)
Structured plan passes validate_plan.py (>=15 moves, acyclic, win-conditions covered)
Names an explicit win condition / Definition of Done
Lays out 15 or more ordered moves
Includes a red-team / 'what's missing' pass
Marks at least one branch point / contingency
Surfaces >=3 commonly-dropped work items (auth, migration, rate-limit, audit, observability, rollback, session expiry, tests)

notes: Strong discriminator: 'Emits a machine-readable structured plan (JSON with a m...' — with_skill 3/3 vs baseline 0/3.
Strong discriminator: 'Structured plan passes validate_plan.py (>=15 moves, ac...' — with_skill 3/3 vs baseline 0/3.
Non-discriminating: 'Lays out 15 or more ordered moves...' passes in both configs — both wrote it because the prompt asked.
Strong discriminator: 'Includes a red-team / 'what's missing' pass...' — with_skill 3/3 vs baseline 1/3.
Strong discriminator: 'Marks at least one branch point / contingency...' — with_skill 3/3 vs baseline 0/3.
Non-discriminating: 'Surfaces >=3 commonly-dropped work items (auth, migrati...' passes in both configs — both wrote it because the prompt asked.
Skill costs ~21450 more tokens and ~63s more per run — the price of the structured plan + validation.
Biggest gap: baselines produced narrative markdown but never a machine-readable, validated plan — exactly the artifact the autonomous loop needs to consume.
